Why tokens keep becoming “not active” (`401 M_UNKNOWN_TOKEN`), and how to reduce it:
Most commonly, access tokens are invalidated when you log out that session/device, “sign out all sessions/devices”, reset the client, or otherwise revoke sessions for the account.
On matrix.org, tokens are generally stable until revoked.
Copying the wrong thing is also common: recovery keys/exported encryption keys are not access tokens, and some UI paths surface different secrets that look token-like.
Switching which account/session you’re using can silently change the token (and then the old one becomes irrelevant/inactive for your bot’s identity).
Ways to make it stop happening (operationally, without adding automation):
Treat the bot as a dedicated account and don’t log it out or “sign out all devices” unless you intend to rotate credentials.
Use a single long-lived session for the bot and copy the access token from that session; avoid repeatedly re-authing the bot in Element.

When you do rotate the token, immediately update both `access_token` and confirm `user_id` matches `whoami` by running `python3 -m gateway.runtime --config config/cce.json --check-connection` (this catches mismatches before you get loops/non-responses).

**What Changed (Phase 3)**
- Added a deterministic workspace runtime with explicit manifests (`workspace.toml`), explicit declared sources, and bounded `.md`/`.txt` reading only.
- Integrated narrow workspace commands into the existing Matrix gateway: `workspace list`, `workspace open <id>`, `workspace status`, `sources`, `read <source>`.

**Files Created**
- `workspace_runtime/__init__.py`
- `workspace_runtime/errors.py`
- `workspace_runtime/manifest.py`
- `workspace_runtime/runtime.py`
- `docs/workspace-runtime.md`
- `docs/workspace-lifecycle.md`
- `docs/source-grounding-principles.md`
- `workspaces/example-workspace/workspace.toml`
- `workspaces/example-workspace/sources/sample.md`
- `workspaces/example-workspace/notes.md`
- `tests/test_workspace_runtime.py`

**Files Modified**
- `gateway/runtime.py` (instantiates `WorkspaceRuntime`, routes workspace commands, adds `workspaces_dir` config default)
- `gateway/status.py` (adds workspace command handler + source/read commands)
- `README.md` (Phase 3 workspace runtime section)
- `docs/decision-log.md` (Phase 3 decisions D-012..D-014)
- `tests/test_gateway_status.py` (updated unknown-command expectation)

**Runtime Behavior Added**
- Workspace discovery: only `workspaces_dir` (default `workspaces/`), only direct children with `workspace.toml`.
- Manifest parsing: strict TOML v1 (`workspace_id`, `title`, optional `description`, `notes`, `sources`, `policies.max_read_bytes`).
- Source enumeration: lists declared sources only; flags missing and unsupported types.
- Source reading: only declared sources, only `.md`/`.txt`, rejects absolute paths and path traversal, enforces `max_read_bytes`.

**Commands Added (via Matrix gateway)**
- `workspace list`
- `workspace open <id>`
- `workspace status`
- `sources`
- `read <source>`

**Tests Run**
- `python3 -m unittest tests.test_gateway_status tests.test_matrix_sync_state tests.test_workspace_runtime`

**Intentionally Deferred**
- Any model cognition/summarization/runtime
- PEM runtime integration
- Shell execution/tools expansion
- Any embeddings/vector DB/search
- Recursive indexing or undeclared file discovery
- Persisting “active workspace” selection across restarts (currently process-local only)

**Architectural Assumptions Made**
- Manifest format for Phase 3 is TOML (`workspace.toml`) to keep parsing stdlib-only (`tomllib`) and avoid adding YAML parser dependencies.
- Workspace truth is strictly “declared sources only”; undeclared paths are rejected even if they exist.

turns.answer:
No — in the current Phase 2/3 implementation the gateway runtime is just a foreground Python process. It only “exists” while you have `python3 -m gateway.runtime --config config/cce.json` running somewhere.

There’s no persistence mechanism yet (no systemd service, launchd job, tmux/screen session, or any watchdog). That’s intentional so far: we’ve kept scope small and inspectable, and we haven’t introduced any long-lived daemon management layer.

If you want persistence next, we can do it in a bounded way (e.g., a minimal `systemd` unit on Colossus or a tmux recipe) without adding new capabilities, but it would be a Phase 4-style operational step rather than Phase 3 workspace foundations.

`--check-connection` now passes with the new token:

- homeserver reachable: yes
- token accepted: yes; `whoami user_id: @cce-matrix:matrix.org`
- room access: yes
- sync ok: yes

Why tokens keep becoming “not active” (`401 M_UNKNOWN_TOKEN`), and how to reduce it:

- Most commonly, access tokens are invalidated when you log out that session/device, “sign out all sessions/devices”, reset the client, or otherwise revoke sessions for the account. On matrix.org, tokens are generally stable until revoked.
- Copying the wrong thing is also common: recovery keys/exported encryption keys are not access tokens, and some UI paths surface different secrets that look token-like.
- Switching which account/session you’re using can silently change the token (and then the old one becomes irrelevant/inactive for your bot’s identity).

Ways to make it stop happening (operationally, without adding automation):
- Treat the bot as a dedicated account and don’t log it out or “sign out all devices” unless you intend to rotate credentials.
- Use a single long-lived session for the bot and copy the access token from that session; avoid repeatedly re-authing the bot in Element.
- When you do rotate the token, immediately update both `access_token` and confirm `user_id` matches `whoami` by running `python3 -m gateway.runtime --config config/cce.json --check-connection` (this catches mismatches before you get loops/non-responses).

`python3 -m gateway.runtime --check-connection` is hitting Matrix APIs and getting `401` with `M_UNKNOWN_TOKEN`, which means “this token is not active / not recognized”. Common reasons this happens on Matrix/Element:
- The token you copied wasn’t actually an access token (e.g., an encryption key/export/recovery key).
- The access token was revoked/expired (log out, “sign out all sessions”, device reset, security action, etc.).
- You replaced the token with another account’s token, so it no longer matches the intended bot identity.

`--check-connection` now passes with the new token:

- homeserver reachable: yes
- token accepted: yes; `whoami user_id: @cce-matrix:matrix.org`
- room access: yes
- sync ok: yes

Why tokens keep becoming “not active” (`401 M_UNKNOWN_TOKEN`), and how to reduce it:

- Most commonly, access tokens are invalidated when you log out that session/device, “sign out all sessions/devices”, reset the client, or otherwise revoke sessions for the account. On matrix.org, tokens are generally stable until revoked.
- Copying the wrong thing is also common: recovery keys/exported encryption keys are not access tokens, and some UI paths surface different secrets that look token-like.
- Switching which account/session you’re using can silently change the token (and then the old one becomes irrelevant/inactive for your bot’s identity).

Ways to make it stop happening (operationally, without adding automation):
- Treat the bot as a dedicated account and don’t log it out or “sign out all devices” unless you intend to rotate credentials.
- Use a single long-lived session for the bot and copy the access token from that session; avoid repeatedly re-authing the bot in Element.
- When you do rotate the token, immediately update both `access_token` and confirm `user_id` matches `whoami` by running `python3 -m gateway.runtime --config config/cce.json --check-connection` (this catches mismatches before you get loops/non-responses).

The `workspace open montana-trip` *did* generate a reply on the gateway side (logs showed `sent reply ...` and I was able to fetch the room message via the Matrix API as `Workspace open: montana-trip`), but Element didn’t surface it to you immediately. That kind of “reply was sent but operator didn’t see it” is exactly the class of thing we should harden.

Most likely causes (given what we observed):
- Element UI sync/delivery delay or timeline not updating promptly.
- The reply got buried by other messages (e.g., model answers) or you were looking at a different room/device view.
- The bot identity/token/user_id mismatch issues we hit earlier can also make “own message” behavior confusing, but in this specific case the gateway did send and did not loop.

If you want, the smallest UX hardening that stays within current scope is:
- Make `workspace open <id>` reply include a short echo of `workspace status` in the same message (so you get immediate confirmation even if you missed the prior line).
- Add a `help` command (purely static text) so conversational “please list workspaces” reliably gets a “use `workspace list`” response instead of going to the model.

Tell me which of those you want (or neither), and I’ll implement it narrowly.

On conversational requests: agreed to treat separately; what happened with “Please list the workspaces” is exactly the current Phase 4.1 routing rule (ordinary text routes to grounded QA when a workspace is active), so we’ll want a deliberate policy for “operational intent” vs “grounded QA” rather than hoping it’s obvious.

Why tokens keep becoming “not active” (`401 M_UNKNOWN_TOKEN`), and how to reduce it:

- Most commonly, access tokens are invalidated when you log out that session/device, “sign out all sessions/devices”, reset the client, or otherwise revoke sessions for the account. On matrix.org, tokens are generally stable until revoked.
- Copying the wrong thing is also common: recovery keys/exported encryption keys are not access tokens, and some UI paths surface different secrets that look token-like.
- Switching which account/session you’re using can silently change the token (and then the old one becomes irrelevant/inactive for your bot’s identity).

Ways to make it stop happening (operationally, without adding automation):
- Treat the bot as a dedicated account and don’t log it out or “sign out all devices” unless you intend to rotate credentials.
- Use a single long-lived session for the bot and copy the access token from that session; avoid repeatedly re-authing the bot in Element.
- When you do rotate the token, immediately update both `access_token` and confirm `user_id` matches `whoami` by running `python3 -m gateway.runtime --config config/cce.json --check-connection` (this catches mismatches before you get loops/non-responses).
